description_long: This triumphal arch is a monumental stone structure featuring a large central archway flanked by two smaller passageways. The exterior is adorned with intricate sculptural reliefs, including figures and decorative motifs, and the upper section is crowned with a detailed cornice and frieze. The surface is predominantly a pale stone, with subtle weathering that highlights the carved details. The arch is hollow, allowing passage through its central and side openings, and the roof is bordered by a balustrade. Prominent inscription panels and ornate pilasters further enhance its classical appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of the Arc de Triomphe in Paris, reflecting a neoclassical architectural style.
description: A grand stone triumphal arch with sculpted reliefs, inscription panels, and a balustraded roof, allowing passage through its central and side arches.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main central archway, flanked by sculpted reliefs and detailed inscription panels.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth of the structure, the balustraded roof, and the side passageway.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with visible sculptural reliefs and the central archway.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, showing the other side passageway and the ornate upper cornice.
